goal: 整合 Claude adapter C3 handoff

summary.title: Integrate Claude C3 filter policy handoff
summary.what: Copied the completed C3 filtering and linearization decision packet into the canonical Claude adapter workflow, marked C3 integrated, and recorded that C4 can now plan the private experimental slice.
summary.why: C4 needs a durable policy for what Claude records may become canonical cxs messages before it can define a safe implementation write set.
summary.user_goal: 整合 Claude adapter C3 handoff

decisions.chose: Accept a narrow allowlist projection for Claude canonical messages.
decisions.rationale: Only top-level non-meta, non-sidechain user and assistant text records fit the existing cxs role model and privacy boundary.

decisions.chose: Keep claude-code reserved and unsupported in public CLI behavior.
decisions.rationale: The current workflow is still a private adapter design path; public source promotion requires a later explicit workflow.

decisions.chose: Mark C4 ready after integrating C1, C2, and C3 handoffs.
decisions.rationale: The evidence, ingestion route, and filtering policy dependencies are now present in the control plane.

rejected.reason: Those payloads are private or semantically unsupported and would violate the current cxs message contract.

review_notes: Actual commit files are C3-filter-linearize.md, session-registry.md, and workflow-state.md.
Mainline prepare shows the full branch diff from origin/main because this is a stacked workflow branch.
Validation: git diff --cached --check passed before commit.
No implementation, public-source promotion, release, global install, push, PR, merge, or production action was performed.

architectural_claims: Claude canonical message projection should be a narrow allowlist of top-level non-meta, non-sidechain user/assistant text records.
Claude tool, thinking, attachment, diagnostics, hook, status, and sidechain payloads must be skipped or kept adapter-private until a later explicit design expands the contract.
C4 can now plan the private experimental adapter slice from C1/C2/C3 evidence.
security_implications: The C3 policy reduces privacy risk by excluding tool outputs, attachments, thinking/signatures, sidechain records, raw paths, diagnostics, and unknown payloads from canonical indexed content.
